hypotheses: The perception of humor is influenced by the type of humor and the emotional context of the audience.
claim.statement: Humor appreciation during Covid-19 is complex and influenced by personality, psychological distance, and humor type.
claim.statement_location: The document's summary section on the study focus and conclusions.

data.wave_or_subset: Survey conducted from March 18 to March 30, 2020
data.notes: Data collection was conducted via social media and email;

method.description: The study collected responses via a survey on emotional reactions to Covid-19 humor, using scales for funniness and aversiveness; personality and psychological distance were assessed.
steps: Collect Internet jokes from humor hubs.
Divide them into Covid-19 and non-Covid-19 categories.
Survey participants on funniness and aversiveness using a 7-point scale.
Assess humor coping and optimism using CHS and LOTR.
Measure psychological distances: infection risk, spatial distance, social circle Covid-19 cases.
method.independent_variables: Humor type, personality measures, psychological distance

results.summary: Covid-19 humor was more aversive than non-pandemic humor. Humor as a coping mechanism affected funniness and aversiveness. Greater perceived risk increased aversiveness, while geographical distance increased humor appreciation.

metadata.original_paper_title: Funny but aversive: A large-scale survey on the emotional response to Covid-19 humor in the Italian population during the lockdown
